title: stylidium insensitivum
intro: Stylidium insensitivum, commonly known as the trigger plant or trigger wort, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Stylidium. It is a perennial plant native to Western Australia and is characterized by its unique trigger-like mechanism that releases a pungent odor when touched. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Stylidium insensitivum can be utilized through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
